hero_subtitle: SFTY supports regulated VASPs with segregation of duties, secure permissions, audit logs, and evidentiary integrity to strengthen governance and readiness for institutional trust.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Role conflicts slow compliance approval workflows
problem_description: Without clear segregation of duties, compliance reviews risk overlapping responsibilities and unclear approvals, which complicates audit traceability. Institutional teams struggle to enforce governance policies and maintain an evidence trail that withstands bank due diligence and regulatory inspection demands.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY structures compliance workflows with precise role-based access controls, segregation of duties, audit logs and decision evidence to support clear governance, stronger traceability and bank due diligence readiness.
workflow_title: From access controls to audit evidence
workflow_description: SFTY enforces role-based permissions that guide compliance reviews, approvals, evidence capture and audit logs for secure and accountable enterprise workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond manual controls. Role-based evidence control
why_sfty_description: SFTY supports segregation of duties by connecting access permissions, audit logs and evidence into structured workflows for clearer compliance governance and institutional trust.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports regulated VASPs requiring controlled review, audit-ready evidence and governed deployment to maintain clear segregation of duties across compliance workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How does SFTY support segregation of duties in compliance
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how review and approval workflows preserve audit trails
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how role-based access strengthens evidence integrity
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY supports bank due diligence packages
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY support segregation of duties?
faq_1_answer: SFTY enforces role-based access and permission controls to support segregation of duties across compliance review and approval workflows.
faq_2_question: How are compliance workflows managed securely?
faq_2_answer: Compliance workflows are structured with controlled case access, decision logging, escalation paths and approval gates to maintain security and governance.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and supporting evidence in audit logs to support review readiness and evidence integrity.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance systems?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects provider outputs and internal data into a connected operating layer without disrupting existing compliance stack components.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but connects their outputs into structured workflows, cases and audit-ready reports.
final_title: Structure compliance with role-based access control
final_description: Review how SFTY supports segregation of duties, audit logs and secure permissions to enhance governance and enterprise readiness.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
